title: cornus sericea f. interior
intro: Cornus sericea f. interior, also known as Red Osier Dogwood, is a shrub species native to North America, characterized by its distinctive red stems and white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of Cornus sericea f. interior include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and immune system booster. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
